acceptance.note: PN248 acceptance distribution not recorded for the kv-auto chat single-stream sweep (greedy-decode workloads have implicit ~100% target-track acceptance). Operator may collect a PN248 sample later via SNDR_ENABLE_SPEC_DECODE_ACCEPTANCE_METRIC=1 and refresh this block; the wall-TPS / TPOT / tool-call measurements below are the load-bearing evidence for the decision.

metrics.note: Single-stream rig sweep bcv4uksw4 (journal §35), pin 0.23.1rc1.dev148+gb4c80ec0f, 2x A5000. kv-auto variant: 70.1 wall TPS, TPOT 10.98 ms, tool-call 7/7. Measured against the TurboQuant chat sibling (gemma4-31b-tq-mtp-chat-k3): 41.4 TPS, TPOT 21.7 ms, tool-call 6/7. Net: kv-auto is +69.6% TPS AND better tool-call quality; the only cost is context 64K (TQ) -> 32K (kv-auto), because uniform fp16 KV is ~4x the turboquant_4bit_nc footprint.

notes: kv-auto chat-role single-stream sweep 2026-06-19 on pin 0.23.1rc1.dev148+gb4c80ec0f. Uniform fp16 KV (no TurboQuant compression, no skip-layers), engine-default attention backend, MTP K=3 via gemma-4-31B-it-assistant. Drafter Triton routing (G4_71B/G4_75) + spec-verify G4_81 (G4_67 disabled) + observability kept; the full TurboQuant infra stack (G4_60A-L, G4_61/62, G4_32, P65, G4_68, G4_69/70, SNDR_G4_TQ_FORCE_SKIP_LAYERS) is dropped because it is meaningless on a uniform-fp16 / non-TURBOQUANT path. Throughput-vs-context sibling of gemma4-31b-tq-mtp-chat-k3 (config_hash aba0bb6b158f7632): operator runs both — TQ owns the 64K long-context slot, kv-auto owns the +70% high-throughput 32K chat slot.
